changes_made: Renamed k8s_admission_controller_vulnerability_policy_configured to k8s_admission_controller_vulnerability_policy_enforced for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to vulnerability scanning and monitoring. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made to improve clarity and consistency with existing patterns. The functions are technically correct and align with the FedRAMP control RA-5 (2) by ensuring images are scanned for vulnerabilities and policies are enforced to prevent vulnerable images from being deployed.
naming_improvements: Use 'enforced' instead of 'configured' to clearly indicate the action taken by the admission controller
title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Update Vulnerabilities to Be Scanned
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP control RA-5 (2) focuses on vulnerability monitoring and scanning, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment and continuously monitored. This involves enabling image vulnerability scanning in registries, configuring admission controllers to enforce policies that prevent vulnerable images from being deployed, and ensuring that images are scanned upon admission to the cluster.
